matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: An observed MTOR variant places this sample in the curated MTOR research context. The local MTOR knowledge base is designed for signaling and research interpretation. Pathogenic MTOR variants do exist in developmental and focal cortical dysplasia syndromes, but the bundled records here focus on commonly discussed regulatory polymorphisms and pathway context. Common MTOR polymorphisms are mainly interpreted as expression or signaling modifiers with context-dependent effect sizes.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: MTOR sits at the center of growth, anabolic signaling, and autophagy control, making it a core aging and cancer pathway gene.
Clinical research links MTOR to immunosuppression, targeted oncology, developmental syndromes, and neurodevelopmental disease.
Common regulatory SNP work is mostly cancer-risk or outcome oriented rather than diagnostic on its own.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a MTOR variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. MTOR methylation is best treated as pathway-regulatory context around a very large signaling locus. Promoter-proximal methylation may reflect transcriptional accessibility, but most interpretation remains pathway level rather than variant specific. The local MTOR knowledge base is designed for signaling and research interpretation. Pathogenic MTOR variants do exist in developmental and focal cortical dysplasia syndromes, but the bundled records here focus on commonly discussed regulatory polymorphisms and pathway context. Common MTOR polymorphisms are mainly interpreted as expression or signaling modifiers with context-dependent effect sizes.

Clinical research links MTOR to immunosuppression, targeted oncology, developmental syndromes, and neurodevelopmental disease.
Common regulatory SNP work is mostly cancer-risk or outcome oriented rather than diagnostic on its own.
